title: Rei in love
wikitext:
[]: Rei in love?             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jul 18, 2004 2:04 pm I don't know if this have been asked before but do you believe that Rei II was in love with Gendo or she just felt affection and gratefulness for saving her(the accident with Eva00).What's your opinion on that?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sun Jul 18, 2004 2:07 pm I think Rei II simply looked upon Gendou as a father figure. For her, the love between a daughter and a father was more than she had ever known so it appeared much more pronounced.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Sun Jul 18, 2004 2:27 pm A while ago, i found a really good opinion on this subject... The first Synch test between Rei and the Eva00, failed because an emotional issue of Rei. This emotional issue must be because she has no reason to live at that point, only as an object for Gendo. But when Gendo rescued her, she thought that gendo really care about her, so gendo became her reason to live. Also Shinji became very important to her later in the series. Rei III didn't had this experience with gendou, maybe she could remember it, but she hadn't that emotional atachment, so she was colder, and broke free from gendo's manipulations.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sun Jul 18, 2004 7:02 pm i think she's capable of loving...just not gendo...he gives her purpose as she calls her "ties"

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Jul 18, 2004 7:07 pm sadsadshinji wrote: i think she's capable of loving...just not gendo...he gives her purpose as she calls her "ties"

[]:             Quote Post by Crazyman42 » Sun Jul 18, 2004 8:27 pm You have to remember as well that Rei was cloned form Yui, this might make her soul have emotional attachments to Gendou.

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Sun Jul 18, 2004 9:10 pm And Shinji!

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sun Jul 18, 2004 9:54 pm I'm inclined to agree with that, there are plenty of studies showing that genetics do play a role in the way an individual behaves.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Jul 18, 2004 10:07 pm I think Rei did have feelings for Shinji. Correct me if im wrong but arn't Shinji and Gendo the only people to show any kindness to Rei the entire show?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 18, 2004 11:01 pm Yes, they are the only ones to show profound kindness to Rei. But I don't think Rei has a really well defined concept of love. I would argue that, like Kaworu, her understanding of that feeling is a more generalized kind of love. Certainly not romantic, but perhaps something of a love between a father and daughter, as is the case of Rei II, and a mother and a child, as is the case with Rei and Shinji. But I've never believed Rei to have any sort of romantic/sexual form of love, but then her words "is this my soul's desire to be one with Ikari?" have to make me question this. As being one with someone has very profound sexual connotations. She says "Dame" which is something along the lines of "Can't" or "No". In the English, she says "You will not". Whether she was replying to her own statement, or telling the Angel it will not harm Shinji is what I would like to know.

[]:             Quote Post by Asterix » Mon Jul 19, 2004 1:01 am DatDude wrote: I think Rei did have feelings for Shinji. Correct me if im wrong but arn't Shinji and Gendo the only people to show any kindness to Rei the entire show?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Jul 19, 2004 2:41 am why does she blush when Shinji tells her that she looks motherly? i think its showing that she has some memories of her past life (as yui) to provoke such a response...perhaps that explains her attachement to gendo and Shinji

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Mon Jul 19, 2004 1:45 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: But I've never believed Rei to have any sort of romantic/sexual form of love, but then her words "is this my soul's desire to be one with Ikari?" have to make me question this. As being one with someone has very profound sexual connotations. She says "Dame" which is something along the lines of "Can't" or "No". In the English, she says "You will not". Whether she was replying to her own statement, or telling the Angel it will not harm Shinji is what I would like to know.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Jul 20, 2004 4:32 am i agree...she seems to be telling the angel that she wont allow it to take Shinji
